title: My Mandate
subtitle: Tell your representatives what you want them to do — refine your ideas with AI and save them as official mandates.

steps.title: Log In
steps.description:
Go to My Mandate from the nav bar. You'll see a login card with two tabs: Phone and Email.

Phone: Enter your 10-digit verified phone number, or tap the mic button to say it aloud. The system reads it back for confirmation.

Email: Type your verified email address, or tap the mic button to say it (e.g. "harley at gmail dot com").

If multiple accounts share your phone number, you'll be asked to enter your first name to disambiguate.

New user? Click "Create Your Account" to sign up via the standard join flow.

steps.title: The Mandate Interface
steps.description:
Once logged in, you'll see the mandate workspace laid out top to bottom:

1. Header — your location and congressional district (e.g. "Putnam, CT — District CT-2") with a graduation-cap "How It Works" link back to this guide
2. Response — where AI responses appear as you chat
3. Prompt — text input with mic and send buttons, where you type or dictate your idea
4. Ideas Scratchpad — pinned ideas with save buttons
5. Public Mandate Summary — saved mandates from your area with filter tabs and a link to The People's Pulse

Click your location line to open a popup showing your elected representatives — U.S. Senators, House Rep, and Governor — with photos, party, phone, and website. The popup is draggable and closeable.

steps.title: Type or Dictate Your Idea
steps.description:
In the Prompt area, type your idea in the text box or click the mic button to dictate by voice. Speak naturally — your words appear in the text box as you talk.

You don't need a polished statement. Just share your raw thoughts: "we need to get big money out of politics" or "fix the potholes on Route 44."

Click the send button (or say "command" then "send" by voice) to submit your idea to the AI.

steps.title: Refine with AI
steps.description:
Your AI response appears in the Response area above the prompt. The AI engages with clarifying questions and helps craft a specific mandate statement. It may:

- Ask which approach you prefer (e.g. constitutional amendment vs. legislation)
- Provide relevant facts and context
- Suggest a polished "Mandate:" statement

Keep the conversation going until you have a mandate you're happy with. You can send multiple messages to refine it.

steps.title: Pin Your Mandate
steps.description:
When the AI produces a mandate statement you like, click the gold pin button on that response. The mandate text is extracted and added to your Ideas Scratchpad below.

Duplicate pins are prevented — if you accidentally pin the same text twice, it tells you it's already pinned.

You can pin multiple ideas from different conversations. They accumulate in the scratchpad until you clear them.

steps.title: Save Your Mandate
steps.description:
In the Ideas Scratchpad, select which pinned idea to save using the dropdown, then click one of the save buttons:

- Save Federal Mandate — for your U.S. congressional representatives
- Save State Mandate — for your state legislators
- Save Town Mandate — for your local town officials
- Save as Idea — save privately without publishing

After saving, the Public Mandate Summary at the bottom updates automatically to show your new mandate alongside others from your district.

steps.title: Voice Commands
steps.description:
With the mic on, say "command" to enter command mode (mic turns gold). Then say any command:

- "send" — submit your dictated text to AI
- "pin" — pin the last AI response
- "save federal mandate" / "save state mandate" / "save town mandate"
- "save idea" — save as private idea
- "read #1" — read back a pinned idea
- "read my mandate" — read your saved mandates aloud
- "delete #3" — remove a pinned idea
- "clear all" — clear everything
- "clear prompt" — clear just the text input
- "clear response" — clear chat but keep pins
- "help" — list all commands

Say "command" again to return to dictation mode.

steps.title: Managing Your Ideas
steps.description:
The Ideas Scratchpad holds your pinned ideas during your session. You can:

- Click the X button to remove an idea
- Use the dropdown to select which idea to save
- Click "Clear All" to remove all pinned ideas and clear the chat

Pinned ideas persist in your browser until you clear them. Saved mandates are stored permanently in the database.

steps.title: Public Mandate Summary & The People's Pulse
steps.description:
Below the scratchpad, the Public Mandate Summary shows saved mandates from your area. Filter tabs inside the box let you narrow the view:

- All — combined federal + state + town mandates
- Federal — mandates for your U.S. congressional district (e.g. "Constituent Mandate for CT-2")
- State — mandates for your state legislators (e.g. "Constituent Mandate for CT")
- Town — mandates for your local town officials (e.g. "Constituent Mandate for Putnam")
- My Mandates — only mandates you have saved, with Federal/State/Town labels on each

The title changes to match the selected filter. Click it or your district info to reopen the delegation popup.

Below the tabs, a "The People's Pulse" link opens the full summary page with topic breakdowns, activity stats, and CSV export — the aggregated voice of your district.

steps.title: Tips
steps.description:
- Be specific: "Pass the DISCLOSE Act" is stronger than "fix campaign finance"
- One idea per mandate: keep each mandate focused on a single action
- Use all three levels: federal, state, and town mandates address different representatives
- Voice works best in a quiet environment
- Your mandates are tied to your location — they go to the right representatives
- Come back anytime to add more mandates as new issues arise
- Check The People's Pulse to see how your mandates contribute to the collective voice of your district
